source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that informed or directionally support each lever's savings range. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.

condition_precedents: IoT / vibration sensors installed on critical assets (OEE impact > ₹1 Cr/year downtime risk)
CMMS / EAM updated with asset criticality classifications (A/B/C)
Maintenance technician training on condition-based protocols completed
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)
Deloitte — Real Estate Predictions 2025 (deloitte.com/insights/real-estate)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Rank assets by criticality (availability impact × replacement cost); instrument top-20 critical assets with vibration, temperature, and oil sensors
Build ML predictive models (anomaly detection, remaining useful life) using historian and CMMS data; validate on holdout failure events
Integrate PdM predictions into CMMS (SAP PM/IBM Maximo); shift maintenance triggers from calendar to condition-based
Track OEE improvement, unplanned-outage reduction, and maintenance cost per MW; rebaseline after 6 months

diagnostic_signals.signal: Unplanned outage rate >5% of planned generation hours for critical rotating equipment
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Condition-based maintenance will reduce forced outage cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Preventive maintenance cost >30% of total maintenance budget with >40% of PMs yielding no defect found
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Optimising PM frequency and shifting to PdM will reduce unnecessary maintenance

diagnostic_signals.signal: Mean time between failures declining YoY for pumps, compressors, or transformers
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Asset degradation trend justifies condition-monitoring investment

required_data_fields: Asset criticality register with replacement cost and availability impact
CMMS maintenance history (work orders, failure codes, costs) ≥3 years
Historian/SCADA data for instrumented assets (vibration, temperature, pressure)
Current PM frequency schedule and inspection outcomes
Unplanned outage log with duration and production impact

condition_precedents: MRO spend classified with ABC criticality and vendor rationalisation target set
Annual rate contracts (ARCs) negotiated with empanelled MRO vendors
Min/max stock norms reset on consumption data (not rule-of-thumb)
industry_sources: Ardent Partners — AP Metrics That Matter 2024 (ardentpartners.com/ap-metrics-that-matter-in-2025/)
McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Aggregate MRO spend from plant stores, purchase orders, and P-cards across all sites; classify by category (mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, safety)
Identify consolidation candidates: categories with >5 vendors and fragmented spend; build RFQ for preferred vendor agreements by category
Negotiate VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) agreements for fast-moving C-class items with top MRO distributors; implement consignment for critical spares
Build MRO catalogue in procurement system; enforce catalogue buying; track MRO cost per site and spend under management monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: MRO vendor count >100 per site with top-20 vendors representing <50% of spend, indicating fragmentation
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation will improve leverage and reduce procurement overhead

diagnostic_signals.signal: MRO spend outside purchase order (P-card or credit card) >20%, indicating maverick purchasing
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Catalogue discipline and VMI will reduce off-contract buying

diagnostic_signals.signal: MRO unit price variance >30% for same item across sites due to decentralised purchasing
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Group contracts will harmonise pricing across plants

required_data_fields: MRO spend by site, category, and vendor (12 months)
Purchase order vs credit card spend split
Price data for common items across sites
Current VMI and consignment arrangements
Critical vs non-critical spares classification

condition_precedents: BEE energy audit and specific energy consumption (SEC) baseline established
Energy conservation measures (ECMs) approved and capex sanctioned
ISO 50001 energy management system roadmap initiated
industry_sources: CEA — Annual Report 2023-24 (cea.nic.in/annual-report/)
DPE — Survey of CPSEs 2023-24 (dpe.gov.in/reports/annual-report)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Commission BEE-certified energy audit for all facilities above PAT cycle threshold; establish SEC (Specific Energy Consumption) baseline by production unit
Prioritise energy efficiency projects by payback period (<3 years): motor replacement, variable frequency drives, compressed air, and lighting retrofit
Execute top-10 projects with investment approval; file PAT cycle designation certificate; install DECs (Designated Energy Consumers) as required
Track SEC monthly vs PAT cycle target; submit BEE annual report; prepare for ESCerts trading if target exceeded

diagnostic_signals.signal: Specific Energy Consumption (SEC) above BEE benchmark for the sector or above PAT cycle target
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Energy efficiency projects will reduce SEC and electricity cost, and avoid PAT penalty

diagnostic_signals.signal: Energy cost as % of COGS >12% for electricity-intensive processes (metal, cement, chemical, glass)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Energy efficiency investment will materially reduce production cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Motor load factor <60% for >30% of installed motor capacity indicating oversized or inefficient motors
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Motor replacement or VFD installation will reduce electricity consumption

required_data_fields: Energy consumption data by fuel type, unit, and process (12 months)
BEE PAT cycle designation status and SEC target
Energy audit report with top-10 efficiency recommendations and investment estimates
Electricity tariff structure and peak demand charges
Motor inventory with load factor and age data

condition_precedents: GST reconciliation between GSTR-2A/2B and purchase register completed
Job work transactions classified correctly (Section 143 compliance)
ERP auto-configured for input tax credit matching and reporting
industry_sources: DPE — Survey of CPSEs 2023-24 (dpe.gov.in/reports/annual-report)
Ardent Partners — AP Metrics That Matter 2024 (ardentpartners.com/ap-metrics-that-matter-in-2025/)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Reconcile GSTR-2A/2B (supplier invoices filed) vs GSTR-3B (ITC claimed) for all returns periods; identify unclaimed ITC by GST head
Investigate ITC gaps: misclassified blocked credits, supplier non-filing, and wrong GST head mapping in ERP; quantify recoverable amount
File DRC-03 or revised GSTR-3B for recoverable credits; engage with vendors who have not filed returns to resolve mismatch
Implement monthly auto-reconciliation in ERP/GSTN portal; update chart of accounts to correctly classify ITC-eligible expenses

diagnostic_signals.signal: ITC claimed as % of total GST paid <80% for input-intensive sectors (manufacturing, construction, logistics) indicating under-claim
diagnostic_signals.confirms: ITC recovery will yield direct cash refund or credit

diagnostic_signals.signal: GSTR-2A/2B vs GSTR-3B ITC mismatch >5% of eligible ITC pool
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Reconciliation exercise will identify recoverable amount

diagnostic_signals.signal: Blocked credit (Schedule II items) incorrectly claimed or ITC-eligible items incorrectly coded in ERP as non-ITC
diagnostic_signals.confirms: GL reclassification will unlock additional ITC

required_data_fields: GSTR-2A/2B data vs GSTR-3B ITC claim (all periods under review)
GL coding for input costs by GST head (ITC eligible vs blocked)
Vendor GSTIN compliance status and filing history
ERP GST configuration and tax code mapping
Prior-year tax authority ITC mismatch notices if any

condition_precedents: Network modelling completed for DC / plant footprint and lane analysis
Carrier panel renegotiated on volume consolidated lanes
Primary freight contracts updated with rate lock and capacity guarantees
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)
Atradius — Payment Practices Barometer Asia-Pacific 2024 (atradius.us/knowledge-and-research/reports/payment-practices-barometer-asia-pacific.html)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build current-state logistics network model: map all origin-destination lanes, carrier rates, volumes, and transit times; calculate cost per tonne-km by lane
Run network optimisation model (linear programming or simulation) to identify depot location, mode mix, and lane consolidation opportunities
Negotiate revised contracts with carriers based on optimised lane volumes; implement route-and-load planning software
Track freight cost per tonne-km, OTIF, and carrier performance monthly; reoptimise annually

diagnostic_signals.signal: Freight cost as % of net sales above sector benchmark with <70% truck utilisation on primary lanes
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Network redesign and load optimisation will reduce freight cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average truck fill rate <75% on primary distribution lanes
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Load consolidation or route optimisation will improve cost per tonne

diagnostic_signals.signal: Depot footprint not reviewed in >5 years despite changes in production locations or customer geography
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Network optimisation will reduce depot and haul costs

required_data_fields: Freight spend by lane, carrier, and mode (12 months)
Shipment volume and weight by origin-destination pair
Current depot locations and lease terms
Truck fill rate and load factor data by lane
Customer geography and delivery frequency requirements

condition_precedents: Packaging engineering review completed (right-weighting / gauge optimisation)
Alternative material / supplier qualification approved by QA and marketing
Tooling and changeover costs factored into net savings calculation
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map packaging specifications for top-50 SKUs: material type, weight per unit, supplier, unit cost, and waste generated
Identify lightweighting opportunities (reduce material per unit) and material substitution (mono-material, recycled content) without compromising shelf life
Qualify alternative packaging suppliers; run structural testing per ISTA/FEFCO standards; obtain retail and logistics sign-off
Roll out optimised specifications; track packaging cost per SKU, waste % per pack, and EPR compliance status monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Packaging cost as % of COGS >8% for categories where packaging intensity is not driven by product fragility or regulatory requirement
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Lightweighting and specification review will reduce packaging cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Primary packaging weight per unit 15–25% above benchmarked competitors for same product category
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Lightweighting to peer specification will reduce material cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: EPR (Extended Producer Responsibility) compliance cost growing due to non-recyclable or multi-layer packaging
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Mono-material or recyclable substitution will reduce EPR liability

required_data_fields: Packaging specification and cost per SKU (material, weight, supplier, unit cost)
Packaging waste percentage and EPR filing data
Competitor packaging specification benchmarks by category
Structural integrity requirements by product type (fragility, shelf life)
Retailer and logistics packaging requirements

condition_precedents: Process standardised and stable (< 3% defect rate) — automation prerequisite
Capex business case approved (typical payback < 3 years)
Workforce transition plan (redeployment / VRS) completed with IR clearance
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)
Gartner — IT Budget Benchmarks 2024 (gartner.com/en/information-technology/insights/it-budget)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Conduct automation opportunity assessment: rank processes by labour intensity, repeatability, defect risk, and automation technology readiness
Build ROI model for top-5 automation investments (robotics, vision systems, AGVs, PLC automation); source vendor quotes and validate cycle-time improvement
Execute automation in phased deployment: pilot on one line, validate OEE improvement, then scale to remaining lines
Track OEE improvement, direct labour reduction per unit, defect rate, and automation ROI monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Direct labour cost per unit of production >15% above peer benchmark for comparable automation intensity
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation investment will reduce direct labour cost per unit

diagnostic_signals.signal: Process defect rate >2% on manual assembly or inspection steps vs <0.5% for equivalent automated process at competitor
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will improve quality and reduce rework cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: OEE <65% for key production lines due to changeover time, minor stoppages, and speed loss
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation of bottleneck steps will improve OEE and throughput

required_data_fields: Direct labour cost per unit by production line and shift
OEE data (availability, performance, quality) by line (12 months)
Defect rate and rework cost by process step
Automation vendor options and capital cost estimates
Production volume forecast for ROI model

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Quality Defect & Waste Reduction (Six Sigma / Lean)
condition_precedents: Cost of poor quality (COPQ) baseline established (scrap, rework, warranty)
Six Sigma / Lean project charters approved for top defect causes
SPC (Statistical Process Control) deployed on critical parameters
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Confirm scope for Quality Defect & Waste Reduction (Six Sigma / Lean): validate baseline and stakeholders
Design target process and RACI
Pilot in one BU/geo and measure effort/cost delta
Scale rollout and update SOPs/training

diagnostic_signals.signal: High FTE effort or cycle time vs benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Process inefficiency is material

required_data_fields: Process volume metrics
FTE effort or cost allocation by process
Baseline cycle times
